message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-registry: add policy.require_attestation_expiry to fail-closed on unset expires_at

`enforce_supply_chain_policy` (lib.rs:1498-1518) iterates AttestationEvidence
entries and rejects only those with `expires_at <= now`. Entries with
`expires_at = None` were silently treated as eternally fresh — a verifier
adapter (Sigstore/TUF/etc.) regression that drops the freshness population
would disable freshness enforcement on every connector under that policy
without any signal.

Add `PolicySection::require_attestation_expiry: bool` (default false,
backwards-compatible) and a new RegistryError::AttestationExpiryMissing
variant. When the policy gate is on, `enforce_supply_chain_policy` rejects
any AttestationEvidence whose expires_at is None up-front, before the
expires_at <= now check.

Two regression tests:
- `supply_chain_policy_rejects_unset_expiry_when_required` exercises the
  fail-closed path
- `supply_chain_policy_unset_expiry_passes_when_not_required` proves
  default behavior is unchanged for operators who haven't opted in

Updated all 16 existing PolicySection literal constructors in
fcp-registry tests + 17 in fcp-manifest tests via scripted edit (no
existing literal had a `..Default::default()` fallback).

Audit context: surveyed fcp-evidence (re-export shell over fcp-core),
fcp-core/supply_chain.rs (SupplyChainSignature.algorithm requires strict
ed25519 equality — no downgrade attack; AttestationMetadata.validate
checks build_finished >= build_started but does NOT check freshness
against current time, by design), and fcp-registry (SigstoreVerifier
trait delegates cert chain validation to the adapter — chain truncation
risk is in the adapter, not in this crate). The expiry-required gate
above is the only concrete shippable fix found.

Build-status note: workspace `cargo check` blocked by the same
unrelated fcp-async-core Debug-bound issue noted in earlier commits.
The PolicySection field addition + literal updates are mechanical;
fcp-registry surface only adds a new error variant + a single check
in enforce_supply_chain_policy + two tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
